pattern: Concatenate words in order and stop early as soon as the running prefix matches the target or visibly diverges from it.
hints: The target must equal the joining of some leading run of the word list, so build that joining piece by piece.
Walk a pointer through the target while consuming whole words; if any character disagrees you can answer false immediately.
Append words until the accumulated string reaches the target's length: succeed only if it equals the target exactly at some word boundary.

edge_inputs.description: prefix formed by first three words
characters match but word boundaries never line up exactly
single word equals the whole target
target ends mid-word so no boundary completes it
target is the full concatenation of all words
single character matches first single-char word
target longer than the entire concatenation

stress.description: random words concatenated, target is a valid prefix to a random boundary half the time
stress.note: mixes guaranteed prefixes, perturbed prefixes, and random strings
